text.title: Project Integration Management — Tools, Services, and References
text.summary: CLI tools, SaaS platforms, best practices, and references for integrated project management.

list.item: git + branch protection — charter and integrated plan as code; PR gates sponsor sign-off
gh / glab — drive change-request issues, link to PRs that update baselines
yq / jq — read/patch integrated/plan.yaml; aggregate dependencies (apt install yq jq)
python-docx / python-pptx / pandoc — render charter, status reports, closure docs to org-required formats
mermaid-cli (mmdc) — render integration diagrams (scope → schedule → cost) and status dashboards
mpxj — read/write MS Project .mpp schedules from the integrated plan (https://www.mpxj.org)
pre-commit — block edits to charter.md or baselines without rationale and sponsor reviewer
make / just — standardize "build status pack" / "render closure" tasks
Microsoft Project + Project for the Web — dominant predictive tool; integrates with Teams + Power BI for status.
Smartsheet — portfolio gantt + dashboards; common integration target (REST API).
Jira with Plans / Advanced Roadmaps — common host; cross-project dashboards via JQL.
Azure DevOps — hybrid host: portfolio Plans + team Boards + Pipelines.
ServiceNow SPM — heavy enterprise PPM with full integration; required where governance is the constraint.
Confluence / Notion — charter, decisions, status home; pair with the work-graph host.
DocuSign / Adobe Sign — charter sign-off, formal acceptance (REST API).
OpenProject / Redmine / Tuleap — self-hosted hosts for regulated/airgapped orgs (REST API).

rule.statement: Run an integration check weekly: scope without schedule? schedule without cost? risks without owners? — automate and open issues on failure.
rule.rationale: Automated consistency checks catch silo drift in days rather than months; the weekly cadence is short enough to fix before downstream teams are affected.

rule.statement: Keep change-control velocity moderate: batch CRs into weekly review — too few hides chaos, too many drowns the integrator.
rule.rationale: Emergency CR streams bypass integrated impact analysis; batching forces discipline while preserving responsiveness.

rule.statement: Train sponsors on what they sign — most integration failures are sponsor-driven (silent scope additions).
rule.rationale: If sponsors do not understand the baseline they approved, every informal "can you just add X" becomes an untracked scope change.

list.item: PMI PMBOK 6e — Integration Management Knowledge Area (templates and processes).
PMI PMBOK 7e — Tailoring + Performance Domains (Planning, Project Work, Delivery, Measurement).
ISO 21500 / 21502 — international guidance on integration and tailoring.
Reinertsen "The Principles of Product Development Flow" — queueing theory for integrated cadences.

Sibling methodologies: change-control/, lessons-learned/, scope-management/, schedule-development/, cost-estimation/, risk-register/, quality-management/, procurement-management/, communications-management/, stakeholder-engagement/
